description: Channel width sweep for AsymmetricPairGenerator (depth=1, motion_hidden=32, embed_dim=6). Tests whether Quantizr's base_ch=36/mid_ch=60 is optimal or if a different width dominates the rate-quality Pareto frontier.
hypothesis: Quantizr's 36/60 is a local optimum chosen for their specific training setup. A wider (48/80) or narrower (24/40) architecture may dominate when rate is properly accounted for. The Pareto frontier should reveal whether quality saturates before rate becomes prohibitive.
success_criteria: At least one config achieves proxy score < 1.30 (current best). Pareto frontier shows clear diminishing returns, identifying the optimal width.
kill_criteria: All configs score worse than medium (36/60) by > 5% — channel width is not the bottleneck.
concern_criteria: Tiny/small configs score within 2% of medium — we are overparameterized and should shrink.

configs.name: tiny
configs.notes: Motion predictor dominates (52%). Tests if renderer can function with minimal capacity.

configs.name: small
configs.notes: Renderer now dominant. Sweet spot for rate-constrained deployment?

configs.name: medium
configs.notes: Quantizr's exact config. Baseline for comparison.

configs.name: large
configs.notes: Extended capacity. Does quality improve enough to justify 64% more rate?

configs.name: xlarge
configs.notes: 3.5x Quantizr's params. Tests if capacity is the limiting factor.

training.notes: Identical to council-approved asymmetric warp config. Only base_ch/mid_ch vary.

deployment.parallelism: sequential (1 T4 at a time to stay in free tier)

expected_insights: Quality saturation point — where more params stop helping
Rate crossover point — where rate penalty exceeds quality gain
Motion predictor sizing — should it scale with renderer?
